mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.0%, it signals a deteriorating labor market that triggers recession fears, compressing equity multiples and driving negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the hazard ratio for negative returns post-threshold is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and consumer credit indicate a synchronized demand-supply shock that erodes corporate profit margins, leading to negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term (INDPRO decline × RSAFS decline) fails to predict negative SPX returns with a positive coefficient in a multivariate regression over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-interest-rate environment, a sustained drop in housing starts acts as a leading indicator of broader economic slowdown, negatively impacting forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Housing starts decline but SPX forward returns remain positive in > 50% of observations, or the predictive power of HOUST vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS levels.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI exceeds 3.5% year-over-year, persistent inflation forces the Fed to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and driving negative short-term returns.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are not significantly negative when CPI triggers the condition, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 1-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust economic activity that supports corporate revenue growth, generating positive equity returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: The rolling correlation between PAYEMS month-over-month changes and 5-day SPX returns falls below 0.1 for two consecutive years, indicating structural decoupling.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid growth in real GDP combined with rising consumer credit indicates an overheating economy that historically precedes equity drawdowns due to anticipated policy tightening and margin compression.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to produce negative SPX returns in the subsequent 20 days more than 40% of the time, or the interaction coefficient in a predictive model is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of tight labor markets, surging industrial production signals supply-side resilience that offsets demand constraints, driving positive equity returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to correlate with positive SPX returns when UNRATE is below 4.5%, or the forward return distribution shows no significant skew toward positive outcomes post-signal.
